name: Running circular at Embalse de Guadalest
name_original: Running circular al Embalse de Guadalest
region_intro: Jogging around Embalse de Guadalest offers routes through a protected natural environment, characterized by its striking turquoise reservoir nestled among towering mountains like the Aitana, Serrella, and Xortà ranges. The terrain varies from paved sections to dirt tracks winding through pine, olive, and almond groves. This area provides a mix of scenic paths suitable for different fitness levels, with options ranging from gentle loops to more challenging ascents. Best jogging routes around Embalse de Guadalest The most popular jogging route is Running circular at Embalse de Guadalest, a 5.9 miles (9.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 1 minute to complete. This moderate route offers continuous scenic views around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local runners is Guadalest Reservoir – Guadalest Reservoir loop from el Castell de Guadalest, a moderate 3.0 miles (4.8 km) path. This loop starts from the historic village, providing views of the reservoir and the castle. Local runners also love the Guadalest Reservoir – Guadalest Reservoir loop from Benimantell, a 14.5 miles (23.3 km) trail leading through the reservoir's varied terrain and surrounding hills, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Jogging around Embalse de Guadalest is defined by its turquoise reservoir, towering mountain backdrops, and diverse pine and olive groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult, longer running routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Embalse de Guadalest are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1000 runners have used komoot to explore Embalse de Guadalest's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes around Embalse de Guadalest?
A: The terrain around Embalse de Guadalest is quite varied, offering a mix of experiences for runners. You'll find both paved sections, providing a smoother surface, and dirt tracks that wind through scenic pine, olive, and almond groves. The area is characterized by its stunning turquoise reservoir nestled among towering mountains like the Aitana, Serrella, and Xortà ranges, providing a beautiful backdrop for your run.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier run?
A: While many routes in the area are classified as difficult, there are options for those seeking a more moderate experience. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872638619/running-circular-at-embalse-de-guadalest" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular at Embalse de Guadalest&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.4 km trail that offers continuous scenic views around the reservoir. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38258627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir – Guadalest Reservoir loop from el Castell de Guadalest&lt;/a&gt;, a 4.8 km path starting from the historic village.

Q: How many running trails are available in the Embalse de Guadalest area?
A: There are over 45 dedicated running routes available around Embalse de Guadalest, catering to various fitness levels and preferences. The komoot community has explored these trails extensively, with over 1000 runners using komoot to discover the area's diverse terrain.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while jogging?
A: The Embalse de Guadalest area is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. The reservoir itself, known for its striking blue-green waters, offers continuous scenic views. You'll also be surrounded by majestic mountains, including the Aitana, Serrella, and Xortà ranges. The picturesque village of Guadalest, a Historic-Artistic Site, and its castle perched atop a rocky crag, offer impressive panoramic views that can be enjoyed during or after your run.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Embalse de Guadalest?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular feature in the area. A highly recommended option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e872638619/running-circular-at-embalse-de-guadalest" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running circular at Embalse de Guadalest&lt;/a&gt;, which provides continuous scenic views around the reservoir. Another great loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38258627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir – Guadalest Reservoir loop from el Castell de Guadalest&lt;/a&gt;, offering views of both the reservoir and the castle.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Embalse de Guadalest?
A: The running routes in Embalse de Guadalest are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 15 reviews. Runners frequently praise the stunning natural beauty, particularly the turquoise reservoir and the dramatic mountain backdrops. The varied terrain, from smooth paved sections to winding dirt tracks through groves, is also a highlight, offering diverse experiences.

Q: Is Embalse de Guadalest suitable for longer, more challenging runs?
A: Absolutely. While there are moderate options, Embalse de Guadalest is particularly well-suited for experienced runners looking for a challenge. Many routes feature significant elevation gains and cover longer distances. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10357269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir – Guadalest Reservoir loop from Benimantell&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 23.3 km trail with substantial ascents, often taking around 3 hours 35 minutes to complete.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Embalse de Guadalest?
A: The Embalse de Guadalest area is generally pleasant for outdoor activities. While specific seasonal recommendations aren't provided, the diverse vegetation of pine, olive, and almond trees suggests that spring and autumn would offer comfortable temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summers can be warm, so early mornings or late afternoons might be preferable. Winters are generally mild, making it a year-round destination for running.

Q: Are there any amenities like cafes or places to eat near the running routes?
A: The picturesque village of Guadalest, a Historic-Artistic Site, is located nearby and offers various amenities. While specific cafes directly on the trails are not mentioned, the village provides options for refreshments and meals, making it a convenient stop before or after your run. Its proximity enhances the overall experience of visiting the area.

Q: Can I find routes that start from the village of Guadalest?
A: Yes, you can. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38258627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir – Guadalest Reservoir loop from el Castell de Guadalest&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.8 km path that conveniently starts from the historic village itself. This allows you to combine your run with exploring one of 'The Most Beautiful Villages in Spain' and its castle.

Q: What makes the Embalse de Guadalest area unique for running?
A: Embalse de Guadalest stands out due to its unique combination of a stunning turquoise reservoir, dramatic mountain backdrops, and a protected natural environment. The area is classified as Non-Developable Land of Special Protection, ensuring a serene and unspoiled atmosphere. This blend of natural beauty, varied terrain, and well-maintained routes makes it an exceptional destination for a rewarding running experience.
tips:
Embassament de Guadalest, the Guadalest reservoir, is certainly one of the most famous reservoirs in the area from Torrevieja to Alicante. Thanks to the beautiful castle in Guadalest with the even more fantastic view of the reservoir, this lake is known to many people and is a very popular tour destination.

Excellent! The tour around the reservoir is a real highlight! 👌🚴‍♀️👏 The path leads around the entire reservoir, and the Castell Guadalest can be viewed from all sides. The route around the lake can either be started over the dam and followed the path along the water or the tour can be started in the mountain village of Beniardá.
